meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use SaaS pricing strategies to overcome a lack of portfolio. An advanced guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional case studies.
content.answer_block: Transform your lack of a portfolio into a high-value asset by building a 'Proof-of-Value' SaaS pricing dashboard. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative logic, you demonstrate technical authority to clients, proving you can build complex, revenue-focused architecture before your first official hire.
content.intro:
The cold silence of an empty portfolio is the freelancer’s greatest adversary. You are trapped in the 'experience paradox': you need clients to build a portfolio, but you need a portfolio to get clients. Most developers waste months building generic clones that scream 'junior' to high-ticket prospects.

It is time to pivot. Instead of building another to-do list, we are going to architect a sophisticated SaaS pricing engine. This isn't just code; it is a demonstration of business logic, edge-case handling, and architectural rigor. By using Antigravity and Claude Code, we move from 'coder' to 'technical partner' in a single afternoon.

steps.title: Define the Business Logic Architecture
steps.description: Before writing code, define the pricing tiers and limits using a schema-first approach. This shows clients you understand the business side of SaaS.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust JSON schema for a SaaS pricing model including tiers, feature flags, usage-based limits, and Stripe-compatible metadata fields.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a TypeScript interface for the pricing schema generated by Antigravity'.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Pricing Engine
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a clean, modular pricing component that handles tier switching and conditional rendering.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React component using Tailwind CSS that displays three pricing tiers, highlights a 'Pro' plan, and includes a toggle for monthly vs annual billing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement logic for yearly discount calculation (20%) and update state management for the billing toggle'.

steps.title: Integrate Edge-Case Validation
steps.description: Professional SaaS apps fail gracefully. We need to handle invalid states and API latency.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a validation utility for the pricing component that handles edge cases like zero-usage limits and expired promotional coupons.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'Run unit tests against the pricing engine to ensure all edge cases are covered with 90% code coverage'.

steps.title: Architect for Scalability
steps.description: Ensure your pricing logic is decoupled from the UI, allowing for future backend migrations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the pricing logic into a custom hook (usePricing) that abstracts the calculation layer from the presentation layer.
steps.claude_code_command: claude refactor 'Extract the pricing calculation logic into a standalone service file for better testability'.

steps.title: Deploy the Proof-of-Value
steps.description: Finalize your build and prepare it for your portfolio site.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a README.md snippet that explains the technical decisions made in this pricing engine, focusing on scalability and performance.
steps.claude_code_command: claude deploy 'Push the project to a public repository and configure a Vercel preview URL for the portfolio showcase'.

pro_tips: Never build a UI without a data schema; clients pay for the logic, not the CSS.
Use Claude Code to document your 'why' in commit messages; it acts as a permanent record of your senior-level thinking.
Always include a 'Usage-Based' pricing tier in your demo; it shows you understand how to scale revenue for clients.
Treat your portfolio projects like production code; include CI/CD pipelines even for simple apps.
When pitching, show the code architecture, not just the finished site—clients love seeing the 'engine under the hood'.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing engine is live, record a 60-second Loom video explaining the architectural decisions and embed it directly into your portfolio next to the live link.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect client to find you. Build the proof they need today and command higher rates tomorrow. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
